Read this file first. It tells you where to find context in this repo.

## Sharp Edges & Invariants

- **Never invent API URIs.** All endpoint-to-resource mappings live in `src/ApiConfiguration.php`. Add new resources there first; the `RequestUriBuilder` reads from this registry at runtime.
- **Resources are deserialized via `sys.type`.** `ResourceBuilder::getSystemType()` maps `data.sys.type` values to mapper classes. Adding a new resource type requires registering it in both `ApiConfiguration` and `ResourceBuilder`.
- **Version header is mandatory for mutations.** `update()`, `publish()`, etc. send `X-Contentful-Version` derived from `sys.version`. Never call a mutation method on a resource that wasn't fetched from the API (version will be `null` and the API will reject it).
- **Uploads use a different host.** `Upload` resources must be sent to `https://upload.contentful.com`, not the standard CMA host. This is declared in `ApiConfiguration` via a `host` key override — do not hard-code the upload host elsewhere.
- **Proxies do not make HTTP calls on construction.** `SpaceProxy` and `EnvironmentProxy` are lazy references. They only hit the API when you call a method on them. Prefer proxies over fetching the full space/environment resource.
- **php-vcr cassettes must be kept in sync.** Integration tests under `tests/Integration/` replay HTTP from `tests/Recordings/`. If you change an API call shape, re-record the cassette — stale cassettes will cause silent test mismatches.
- **Backward compatibility is enforced.** `roave/backward-compatibility-check` runs in CI. Any public API change (method signature, removed class, changed return type) will be flagged. BC breaks require a major version bump and a `CHANGELOG.md` entry.
- **PHP minimum is 8.0.** Do not use PHP 8.1+ syntax (enums, fibers, intersection types) without bumping the `composer.json` platform requirement and a changelog entry.
- **`nikic/php-parser` is optional (suggested).** The code generator (`src/CodeGenerator/`) requires it, but the rest of the SDK does not. Guard generator code paths against its absence.

## Key Conventions

- **Commit format:** No strict enforced format in CI; conventional commits are used informally (see git log). Use `type: summary` style.
- **Branch strategy:** `master` is stable. Feature/fix branches are short-lived; no enforced naming pattern.
- **Test location:** `tests/Unit/` mirrors `src/`; `tests/Integration/` and `tests/E2E/` are structured by resource type.
- **Namespace:** `Contentful\Management\` → `src/`; `Contentful\Tests\Management\` → `tests/` (PSR-4 via Composer).
- **Code style:** enforced by `friendsofphp/php-cs-fixer` (config in `.php-cs-fixer.php`); run `vendor/bin/php-cs-fixer fix` to auto-fix.

## Integration Points

**Upstream (this repo consumes):**
- `contentful/core` — base HTTP client, resource builder, link resolution
- Contentful CMA (`https://api.contentful.com`) — all management operations
- Contentful Upload API (`https://upload.contentful.com`) — binary uploads

**Downstream (consumes this repo):**
- PHP application developers via Packagist (`contentful/contentful-management`)
- `contentful/contentful-laravel` and `contentful/ContentfulBundle` — framework integrations

## Build & Quality

```bash
# Run from inside the dev container
composer install &&
  composer run lint-static-analysis &&
  composer test-quick-fail &&
  composer run test-for-bc-breaks
```
